place: Beaver Dam Lake CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Beaver Dam Lake was drained from 2019 to 2020, one year longer than planned because the spillway replacement wasn’t included in the original schedule.
The lake is a man‑made reservoir, built by damming tributaries of Moodna Creek in eastern Orange County.
Water from Beaver Dam Lake flows south into Moodna Creek, which eventually joins the Hudson River between New Windsor and Cornwall‑on‑Hudson.
Since 2009, the lake has been part of the Citizen Statewide Lake Assessment Program, tracking nutrient levels, algae blooms, and invasive species.
A 1991 bathymetric survey found Beaver Dam Lake’s deepest point at 26 feet, a depth that has remained consistent over the past three decades.
The CDP of Beaver Dam Lake surrounds the lake itself and borders the village of Washingtonville to the west and Salisbury Mills to the south.
